channel.title: bothSidesHeadlinesNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from bothSidesHeadlinesNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: ADHD-related traits may intensify chronic pain through anxiety and negative thinking
item.description: A new study, led by researchers from the University of Tokyo, involving nearly 1,000 patients in Japan suggests that attention deficit hyperactivity d...

item.title: Minimally invasive treatment demonstrates sustained improvement for heart valve patients with limited options
item.description: Findings from the AltaValve Early Feasibility Study suggest that patients with a leaky mitral valve have sustained clinical benefits one year followin...

item.title: Diabetes linked to early Alzheimer's-related changes in the blood of Latino adults
item.description: Researchers from the University of California San Diego have found that diabetes is linked to changes in measurable biological signals in the blood th...

item.title: Unraveling the evolution of leukemia in children with Down syndrome
item.description: It may be possible to identify which pre-cancerous cells will develop into a rare type of blood cancer, due to new research showing that a single gene...

item.title: Shock initiative demonstrates over 70% survival in patients with cardiogenic shock
item.description: Findings from the Can Escalation Reduce Acute Myocardial Infarction Mortality in Cardiogenic Shock (CERAMICS) registry demonstrate that early use of a...

item.title: RHR: The GLP-1 Blind Spot: What Ozempic Won’t Do for Your Metabolic Health
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris explores the science behind GLP-1 medications, including their powerful effects on appetite, weight ...

item.title: RHR: Shaking Up the Salt Myth
item.description: For decades, public health guidelines have warned that reducing sodium intake is essential for preventing high blood pressure and cardiovascular disea...

item.title: RHR: Mouth Taping: Beyond the Hype—What the Science Really Says
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris Kresser explores the viral trend of mouth taping and explains why the reality is more complex than t...

item.title: RHR: Erythritol: The ‘Safe’ Sweetener That’s Anything But
item.description: Erythritol, a popular sweetener found in countless sugar-free and keto products, has long been considered a safe alternative to sugar. This episode of...

item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris Kresser is joined by neuroscientist and physician Dr. Tommy Wood to explore how cognitive function c...
